title: Chionanthus oblanceolatus
taxonomy: Chionanthus oblanceolatus belongs to the family Oleaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the olive or ash family. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics. The genus Chionanthus is a part of the subfamily Fraxinoideae. uses: Chionanthus oblanceolatus, commonly known as the Chinese fringe tree, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's leaves and bark are used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
